sections.title: National Environmental Satellite, Data, and Information Service (NESDIS)
Air Traffic Control Systems Command Center (ATCSCC)
Airport Traffic Control Tower (ATCT) and Terminal Radar Approach Control (TRACON), Central Radar Approach (CERAP)
Weather Products versus Weather Elements and Phenomena
Summaries of Specific Weather Information Contained in Various Weather Products
Heat Imbalances Between Earth’s Surface and the Atmosphere
Air Masses, Fronts, and the Wave Cyclone Model
Vertical Motion Effects on an Unsaturated Air Parcel
Vertical Motion Effects on a Saturated Air Parcel
Using a Parcel as a Tool to Evaluate Stability
Vertical Air Motion Effects on Stability
Diurnal Temperature Variation Effects on Stability
Visual Indicators of Orographic Wind Fields
Remnants of Polar Fronts and Shear Lines
The Effects of Orographic Winds and Turbulence on Aviation Operations
Necessary Ingredients for Thunderstorm Cell Formation
The Sun—Prime Source of Space Weather
The Sun’s Energy Output and Variability
Effects of Space Weather on Aircraft Operations
Radiation Exposure to Flightcrews and Passengers
Recency of Observed Elements at Automated Stations
Automated Surface Observing System (ASOS) and Automated Weather Observing System (AWOS)
Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR) and Aviation Selected Special Weather Report (SPECI)

We recognize that in today’s world, an increasing amount of analyzed weather information is available from multiple sources. These sources provide a wide range of meteorological data, such as surface observations, radar, and satellite imagery, and use...
Analyzed Surface Weather information refers to weather data from sources other than the currently approved ground-based ASOS and AWOS weather reporting systems. Alternate sources may include:
AIRMET Issuance Times and Valid Periods
FB Wind and Temperature Aloft Forecast
Graphical FB Wind and Temperature Aloft Forecast
Generic Format of the Forecast Text of an NWS-Prepared TAF.
Aviation Surface Forecast and Aviation Clouds Forecast
Localized Aviation Model Output Statistics (MOS) Program (LAMP)
Corridor Integrated Weather System (CIWS) and Consolidated Storm Prediction for Aviation (CoSPA)
